meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't validate ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't validate ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Validate AI Ideas Faster
content.seo_description: Stop guessing and start building. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to validate your AI ideas in record time.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template provides a production-ready scaffolding that bridges the gap between raw ideation and functional MVP. By combining this modular architecture with Claude Code's autonomous execution, you can rapidly deploy, iterate, and validate your core value proposition without getting bogged down in boilerplate configuration or technical debt.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, paralyzed by the 'idea graveyard'—that space where promising concepts go to die because you spent three weeks setting up a database schema instead of testing your core hypothesis. The industry doesn't reward the best architects; it rewards the fastest learners who can prove their utility to the market.

Today, we kill the hesitation. We are using the Antigravity project template to bypass the friction of initial setup, allowing you to focus purely on the logic that makes your app indispensable. With Claude Code acting as your force multiplier, you will transform a raw thought into a deployable, testable reality in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Set up the foundational directory structure that separates concerns and ensures type safety from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity standard template with integrated TypeScript, Zod validation, and Supabase auth support.

steps.title: Define the Core Hypothesis Schema
steps.description: Use Zod to explicitly define what success looks like for your data model, preventing 'scope creep' during development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Zod schema for my core feature set that enforces strict input validation for the user's primary input.

steps.title: Inject Business Logic via Claude
steps.description: Shift the heavy lifting to the AI, keeping your codebase clean and modular by delegating service layer implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the service layer logic for the user-defined hypothesis, ensuring error handling is built-in.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement business logic in src/services/engine.ts'

steps.title: Automate Endpoint Integration
steps.description: Expose your logic via secure API routes, leveraging the template's built-in middleware for rate limiting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Next.js API route that consumes the core service and returns a validated JSON response.

steps.title: Stress-Test with Synthetic Data
steps.description: Validate your idea by programmatically generating edge-case inputs to see how your app handles failure states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that generates synthetic edge-case data to validate the robustness of the service layer.

pro_tips: Treat your Zod schemas as your product requirements document; if it isn't in the schema, it isn't in the MVP.
Use Claude Code's context awareness by keeping your 'README.md' updated; it acts as the source of truth for the AI's future edits.
Always modularize your logic into services; if a file exceeds 200 lines, split it before asking the AI to refactor it.
Prioritize error handling for the 'happy path' first, but use the Antigravity middleware to catch edge cases globally.
Commit after every successful Claude Code task; this creates a 'rollback safety net' that allows you to experiment fearlessly.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel or Railway, gather three real-world users, and iterate based on their feedback loop.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to outpace the competition. Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
